name: cli
context: Firebase CLI (firebase-tools) for project initialization, emulator suite, deployment, hosting channels, and local development workflows
info: The Firebase CLI (firebase-tools) is the command-line interface for managing Firebase projects end-to-end. Install globally with `npm install -g firebase-tools`. Authenticate interactively with `firebase login` or generate CI tokens with `firebase login:ci` (sets FIREBASE_TOKEN for non-interactive pipelines). Manage projects with `firebase use PROJECT_ID` to switch active projects and `firebase projects:list` to enumerate all projects. Initialize services with `firebase init` which interactively scaffolds firebase.json, security rules, and functions directories. Deploy everything with `firebase deploy` or target specific services with `--only hosting`, `--only functions:myFunc`, or `--only firestore:rules`. The Emulator Suite (`firebase emulators:start`) runs local instances of Auth, Firestore, Functions, Hosting, Storage, and Pub/Sub with a unified UI at localhost:4000, supporting data import/export between sessions. For CI/CD pipelines, authenticate via FIREBASE_TOKEN environment variable or use Google Cloud service account credentials. Deploy targets let you map multiple hosting sites to named targets for multi-site projects.
body:
# Firebase CLI

The Firebase CLI (`firebase-tools`) is the primary command-line tool for initializing, developing, testing, and deploying Firebase projects.

## Installation

```bash
# Install globally via npm
npm install -g firebase-tools

# Verify installation
firebase --version
```

## Authentication

```bash
# Interactive login (opens browser)
firebase login

# Generate a CI token for non-interactive environments
firebase login:ci
# Prints a token — set it as FIREBASE_TOKEN in your CI environment

# Login with a specific account
firebase login --reauth

# Logout
firebase logout
```

For CI/CD pipelines, set the `FIREBASE_TOKEN` environment variable with the token from `firebase login:ci`. Alternatively, use Google Cloud service account credentials by setting `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` to the path of a service account JSON key file.

## Project Setup

```bash
# Interactive project initialization — select services (Firestore, Functions, Hosting, etc.)
firebase init

# Add a project alias
firebase use --add

# Switch active project
firebase use PROJECT_ID

# List all available projects
firebase projects:list

# List Firebase apps in the current project
firebase apps:list
```

`firebase init` creates `firebase.json` (service configuration and deploy settings) and `.firebaserc` (project aliases). Select individual services during init or run `firebase init` again to add more.

## Key Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `firebase init` | Initialize project — select and configure Firebase services |
| `firebase deploy` | Deploy all configured services to production |
| `firebase deploy --only hosting` | Deploy only hosting |
| `firebase deploy --only functions:myFunc` | Deploy a specific Cloud Function |
| `firebase emulators:start` | Start the full Emulator Suite |
| `firebase emulators:start --only firestore,functions` | Start specific emulators only |
| `firebase hosting:channel:deploy NAME` | Create a preview channel for hosting |
| `firebase hosting:rollback` | Rollback hosting to previous deployment |
| `firebase functions:log` | View Cloud Functions logs |
| `firebase firestore:indexes` | List Firestore composite indexes |
| `firebase auth:export users.json` | Export authentication users to JSON |
| `firebase auth:import users.json` | Import authentication users from JSON |
| `firebase use PROJECT_ID` | Switch active project |
| `firebase projects:list` | List all Firebase projects |
| `firebase serve` | Start local development server |
| `firebase apps:list` | List Firebase apps in current project |

## Emulator Suite

The Firebase Emulator Suite runs local instances of Firebase services for offline development and testing.

```bash
# Start all configured emulators
firebase emulators:start

# Start specific emulators
firebase emulators:start --only auth,firestore,functions

# Import data from a previous export on startup
firebase emulators:start --import=./emulator-data

# Export emulator data on shutdown
firebase emulators:start --export-on-exit=./emulator-data

# Import and auto-export for persistent local data
firebase emulators:start --import=./emulator-data --export-on-exit
```

### Default Emulator Ports

| Emulator | Default Port |
|----------|--------------|
| Emulator UI | 4000 |
| Auth | 9099 |
| Firestore | 8080 |
| Functions | 5001 |
| Hosting | 5000 |
| Storage | 9199 |
| Pub/Sub | 8085 |
| Database (RTDB) | 9000 |

Ports are configured in `firebase.json` under the `emulators` key:

```json
{
  "emulators": {
    "auth": { "port": 9099 },
    "firestore": { "port": 8080 },
    "functions": { "port": 5001 },
    "hosting": { "port": 5000 },
    "storage": { "port": 9199 },
    "ui": { "enabled": true, "port": 4000 }
  }
}
```

The Emulator UI at `http://localhost:4000` provides a dashboard for inspecting Auth users, Firestore documents, Functions logs, and Storage files. Emulator data is ephemeral by default — use `--import` and `--export-on-exit` to persist data across restarts.

## CI/CD Usage

Authenticate in CI/CD pipelines without interactive login:

```bash
# Option 1: FIREBASE_TOKEN (legacy but widely used)
export FIREBASE_TOKEN=$(firebase login:ci)  # Run once locally, save the token
# In CI:
FIREBASE_TOKEN=${{ secrets.FIREBASE_TOKEN }} firebase deploy

# Option 2: Google Cloud service account (recommended)
export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=service-account.json
firebase deploy

# Deploy only on main branch merges
firebase deploy --only hosting --message "Deploy from CI - commit $COMMIT_SHA"
```

## Deploy Targets (Multi-Site Hosting)

Deploy targets map multiple hosting sites to named targets for projects with more than one site:

```bash
# Register a deploy target
firebase target:apply hosting app my-app-site
firebase target:apply hosting blog my-blog-site

# Configure in firebase.json
# {
#   "hosting": [
#     { "target": "app", "public": "dist/app", "rewrites": [...] },
#     { "target": "blog", "public": "dist/blog" }
#   ]
# }

# Deploy a specific target
firebase deploy --only hosting:app
firebase deploy --only hosting:blog
```

sections.title: Installation
sections.body: Install globally with `npm install -g firebase-tools`. Verify with `firebase --version`. The CLI is a Node.js package requiring Node 18+. Update with `npm update -g firebase-tools`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.body: Use `firebase login` for interactive browser-based auth. Use `firebase login:ci` to generate a token for CI/CD pipelines — store it as the FIREBASE_TOKEN environment variable. For GCP-native auth, use a service account JSON key via GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS. Revoke credentials with `firebase logout`.

sections.title: Project management
sections.body: `firebase init` interactively selects and configures services (Firestore, Functions, Hosting, Storage, Emulators). `firebase use PROJECT_ID` switches the active project. `firebase use --add` registers a new project alias in .firebaserc. `firebase projects:list` shows all projects. `firebase apps:list` enumerates apps in the current project.

sections.title: Deployment
sections.body: `firebase deploy` pushes all configured services. Use `--only` to target specific services: `--only hosting`, `--only functions`, `--only firestore:rules`, `--only functions:myFunc`. Preview channels (`firebase hosting:channel:deploy NAME`) create temporary URLs for testing. Rollback hosting with `firebase hosting:rollback`. Add `--message` to annotate deployments.

sections.title: Emulator Suite
sections.body: `firebase emulators:start` launches local instances of Auth (9099), Firestore (8080), Functions (5001), Hosting (5000), Storage (9199), and Pub/Sub (8085). The Emulator UI at port 4000 provides a dashboard. Use `--import` and `--export-on-exit` to persist data across sessions. Configure ports in the `emulators` key of firebase.json. Start specific emulators with `--only firestore,functions`. Connect client SDKs to emulators using `connectAuthEmulator()`, `connectFirestoreEmulator()`, etc.

sections.title: CI/CD usage
sections.body: For non-interactive deployment in CI/CD, authenticate with FIREBASE_TOKEN (from `firebase login:ci`) or GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS (service account JSON key). FIREBASE_TOKEN is legacy but widely supported; service account auth is recommended for new setups. Use `firebase deploy --only hosting --message 'CI deploy'` to annotate. Deploy targets support multi-site hosting in a single project.

links.context: Firebase ecosystem overview — all services and project structure

related.note: Firebase ecosystem context — understand project structure before using the CLI
firebase.json and .firebaserc are managed by the CLI
gcloud CLI manages the underlying GCP project; service account auth works across both CLIs

author: dojo-community
